body_preview:
Dear Larry,

Simone called me asking if we might be able to host her engagement party at our place in late July. Your dad thinks the backyard would be nice if we cleared it out a bit. Before I said yes, I wanted to check with you—would it clash with any o
subject: Dear Larry

body_preview:
Hi Mom,

Late July should be fine—I can check my calendar, but I don't think I have anything locked in that week. And honestly, yeah, I could probably handle some of the photography if Simone wants it. She's been so present for me over the years, it'd be
subject: Re: Hi Mom

body_preview:
Dad,

Got the call yesterday — the Asheville wedding we talked about in May came through. It's October, smaller event, probably fifty people max. Good pay, relaxed couple, venue has real light in the late afternoon which is honestly the best thing I could
subject: Dad

body_preview:
Son,

That's great news on the Asheville gig. October light up there is golden, no pun intended. You already know you're good at the work — this is just logistics.

For the portraits, here's what I'd tell anyone nervous about morning shoots: manage the va
subject: Re: Son

body_preview:
Yeah, that tracks. I think I was turning it into something bigger than it is.

Already scouted the venue website — there's a covered porch on the north side that gets soft light until about 10 AM. I'll set up there, do a quick prep with the family, and mo
subject: Re: Yeah, that tracks. I think I was turning it into something b

body_preview:
Hi Larry,

Following up on a recent shared concern. Could we sync this week?

Thanks,
Simone
subject: Hi Larry

body_preview:
Hi Simone,

Thanks for the note. Tomorrow afternoon works — does 3pm fit?

Best,
Larry
subject: Re: Hi Simone

body_preview:
3pm is great. Sending the calendar invite now.

Simone
subject: Re: 3pm is great. Sending the calendar invite now

body_preview:
Got it, see you then.

Larry
subject: Re: Got it, see you then

body_preview:
Hey Terrell,

Dad's turning 65 in July and Mom wants to do something special. She's been dropping hints about getting a new family portrait done. I'm thinking a relaxed shoot at the old spot near the creek—somewhere that doesn't feel too stiff. Could prob
subject: Hey Terrell

body_preview:
Larry,

Definitely count me in. The week of the 14th is probably best for me, but I can make most dates work that month. Mid-July is clear.

One thing though—can you give Dad a heads up that you're doing this? You know how he gets about photos. He'll spen
subject: Re: Larry

body_preview:
Hey Larry,

Caught your message about Simone's shoot. These things happen, seriously — I've had plenty of sessions where the light betrays me or the couple isn't vibing with each other. The fact that you're stressing about it tells me it matters to you, w
subject: Hey Larry

body_preview:
Nina,

Thanks. Yeah, I'm definitely in my own head, but also the light was genuinely trash. Shot at that overlook spot off Independence, and we got there right when the clouds rolled in. By the time we cleared them, we'd lost an hour and everyone was tigh
subject: Re: Nina

body_preview:
Larry,

That's the question, isn't it. Here's what I know after fifteen years: the work feels good when you can look at it three weeks later and still see why you were excited in the moment. Not perfect. Not "correct." Just honest. The fact that Simone's
subject: Re: Larry

body_preview:
Subject: Engagement timeline — when's good for you?

Hey Dev,

Simone mentioned you guys are locking in engagement photos before the holidays. I've got some December openings if that's realistic—nothing booked those first two weeks.

Just checking: this i
subject: Subject: Engagement timeline — when's good for you

body_preview:
Subject: Re: Engagement timeline — when's good for you?

Hey Larry,

Haha, fair question. Yeah, I actually do want this. Simone obviously does too, but I've seen your work through her phone about a thousand times—she makes me look at your portfolio more t
subject: Re: Subject: Re: Engagement timeline — when's good for you

body_preview:
Subject: Re: Engagement timeline — when's good for you?

Okay good. And hey—nobody likes being in front of the camera, trust me. Half my job is making people forget the camera's there. Nervous people usually shoot the best because they're actually *presen
subject: Re: Subject: Re: Engagement timeline — when's good for you
